channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 predictions for emerging ’25 technology trends
item.description: Click on the TV or stream your latest newscast and there is a good chance you’ll hear AI mentioned in some fashion. It’s clear AI remains prevalent today just as it has been for the past several years.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: HPE Aruba targets retailers with IoT, edge computing launches
item.description: HPE Aruba Networking has rolled out a variety of new products for retailers that are aimed at boosting connectivity for in-store and IoT networks, improving analytics, and streamlining management.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CISA publishes security goals for software development process, product design
item.description: The US Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has published IT sector-specific goals (IT SSGs) to protect against cyber threats, including 11 software development process goals and seven product design goals.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 800,000 VW electric vehicle owners' data exposed by software bug
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on a Volkswagen data leak that exposed the personal data of around 800,000 EV owners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to watch Diddy: The Making of a Bad Boy online from anywhere – stream Sean Combs documentary
item.description: The downfall of a big star, as told by those close to him – here's how to watch Diddy: The Making of a Bad Boy online no matter where in the world you are.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Bezos 'very optimistic' about Trump's space agenda
item.description: Jeff Bezos, owner of aerospace company Blue Origin, expressed optimism about the incoming Trump administration's space policy, emphasizing Sunday he is not concerned about competitor Elon Musk's allyship with the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Adobe’s Firefly ‘Bulk Create’ lets users edit thousands of images at once
item.description: Adobe has unveiled a new Firefly “bulk create” feature that lets content designers make changes to thousands of images once, automating actions such as resizing assets and replacing backgrounds at scale.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rumored prices for third-party RTX 5080 graphics cards soar above Nvidia’s MSRP, and I’m worried entry-level models will sell out in a flash
item.description: If purported prices from a retailer are correct, Gigabyte will only have one RTX 5080 model at MSRP – and the others will hike asking prices considerably.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New Samsung Galaxy S25 render leak shows the changes from the S23 Ultra and S24 Ultra
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra is likely to look a little different from the handsets that preceded it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biden's administration proposes new rules on exporting AI chips, provoking an industry pushback
item.description: The Biden administration is proposing a new framework for the exporting of the advanced computer chips used to develop artificial intelligence, an attempt to balance national security concerns about the technology with the economic interests of producers and other countries.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Marvel Zombies lands an October release date on Disney Plus, but it won't debut or end on Halloween
item.description: Marvel Zombies, the MCU's first R-rated animated show, will make its Disney Plus bow in early October.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: 10 top priorities for CIOs in 2025
item.description: As 2025 dawns, CIOs face an IT landscape that differs significantly from just a year ago. AI, once viewed as a novel innovation, is now mainstream, impacting just about facet of the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Steve Bannon says he will 'take down' the 'truly evil' Elon Musk
item.description: President-elect Trump’s former adviser Steve Bannon has vowed to “take down” tech billionaire Elon Musk, calling him “truly evil.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Best cutting-edge health and fitness tech from CES 2025
item.description: CES 2025 showcased everything from artificial intelligence-powered sleep aids to smart ear-cleaning devices. Kurt the CyberGuy has the details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CES was a giant exercise in AI gaslighting
item.description: For a reporter covering CES, the booth for Samsung Display can seem like a pure distillation of what the tech trade show is all about.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: How AI is helping an Israeli TV reporter speak clearly again
item.description: When a renowned Israeli TV journalist lost his ability to speak clearly because of ALS, he thought his career might be over.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This video company is positioning itself for a world without TikTok
item.description: As TikTok pleads with the U.S. Supreme Court to let it continue operating in the United States, one video company is taking advantage of the uncertainty around TikTok’s sister app.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg says Biden officials would 'scream,' 'curse' at Meta team over COVID takedown requests
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said Biden administration officials would “scream” and “curse” at his employees when they disagreed with the government’s takedown requests over pandemic-related content.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment
item.description: Agencies are dedicating more time and staff to digital accessibility efforts, but acquisition is still a key hurdle when it comes to Section 508 compliance. The post Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: X will now label parody accounts
item.description: The social platform X says it will now label parody or satire profiles to distinguish these accounts and boost transparency on the platform. The new labels will be attached to both parody profiles and their posts, X's Safety Team wrote in a statement late Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meta puts the ‘Dead Internet Theory’ into practice
item.description: Meta’s mission statement is to “build the future of human connection and the technology that makes it possible.” According to Meta, the future of human connection is basically humans connecting with AI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CompTIA bolsters penetration testing certification
item.description: CompTIA recently upgraded its PenTest+ certification program to educate professionals on cybersecurity penetration testing with training for artificial intelligence (AI), scanning and analysis, and vulnerability management, among other things.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta makes major move back to free speech and ends 3rd-party fact-checking program
item.description: Meta recently announced it will cease working with third-party fact-checking organizations to flag misleading posts on Instagram, Facebook and Threads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: .NET Minimal APIs: A Game-Changer for Cloud-Native Applications
item.description: .NET Minimal APIs revolutionize cloud-native app development with lightweight, scalable, and secure endpoints. Discover how to build, deploy, and manage minimal APIs in your .NET projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Federal Cyber Leaders Share Innovation Strategies | Federal News Network
item.description: Federal cyber leaders share their strategies for delivering innovation in the federal government, from collaboration and automation to AI and machine learning.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization
item.description: In today's fast-paced digital landscape, effective government IT modernization is crucial for agencies to achieve their missions. The post Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Lemon8 Rises Amid TikTok Ban Uncertainty
item.description: Lemon8, a short-form video app owned by ByteDance, gains popularity in the US ahead of a looming ban on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 5 ways data teams must lead in AI-driven organizations
item.description: Generative AI has inspired a surge of interest in using data to improve the accuracy of business decisions.
item.dc:creator: Info World
